LAW AND MOTION, 302, SMIS AND RABINS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTIONS 2023.030(A) AND 2030.300(D), ARE DENIED. THE PRINCIPAL COMPLAINT OF THE MOVING PARTIES IS THAT WHILE THE RESPONSES IDENTIFY CATEGORIES OF DAMAGES SUSTAINED, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT STATED THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF EACH CATEGORY OF DAMAGES IDENTIFIED. HOWEVER, THE PLAIN WORDING OF THE INTERROGATORIES DO NOT ASK FOR SPECIFIC DOLLAR AMOUNTS OF DAMAGES; THEY INQUIRE INSTEAD AS TO "WHAT DAMAGES' HAVE [BEEN] SUFFERED" AND SUPPORTING FACTS. THE RESPONSES ADEQUATELY AND IN SOME DETAIL IDENTIFY THE CATEGORIES OF DAMAGES CLAIMED TO HAVE BEEN SUFFERED AND THE FACTUAL BASIS FOR THE CLAIM THAT DAMAGES HAVE BEEN INCURRED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185; DEPT. 302. =302/HK

Smis And Rabins Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D)
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS RABIN WORLDWIDE, INC. AND SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D) - The motion and the request for sanctions are denied. The principal complaint of the moving parties is that while the responses identify categories of damages sustained, Plaintiff has not stated the dollar amount of each category of damages identified. However, the plain wording of the interrogatories do not ask for specific dollar amounts of damages; they inquire instead as to "what damages' have [been] suffered" and supporting facts. The responses adequately and in some detail identify the categories of damages claimed to have been suffered and the factual basis for the claim that damages have been incurred. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk

ATTY. IVO KELLER OF THE BUCHALTER NEMER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 227-0900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Unredacted Copies Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions - Plaintiff's motion is granted. Ms. Weissman's communications with other Defendant-companies are not protected by the attorney-client privilege. Defendants failed to meet their burden to demonstrate that the dominant purpose of Ms. Weissman's relationship with the other Defendant-companies was that of attorney and client. The additional documents required by this order shall be served on Plaintiff no later than March 16, 2015. Both parties met their meet-and-confer obligations and Defendants' opposition to Plaintiff motion was not without substantial justification. Accordingly, both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge : Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ilse Scott, Esq., 415.882.7770, for plaintiff Precision Industrial Constructors, Inc.
Glenn Zwang, Esq., 415.227.0900, for defendants Snowflake Mill Investors, LLC, Rabin Worldwide, Inc. and Ryan Smith.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTS SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC, RABIN WORLDWIDE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION AND RYAN SMITH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained without leave to amend under CCP 1061. The complaint complains about past wrongs and not a present controversy between the parties. Plaintiff can redress the alleged wrongs through its other causes of action. This shall be without prejudice, should plaintiff wish to file a motion for leave to amend complaint. The demurrer to the injunctive relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead that claim as a remedy/prayer for relief. The demurrers to the two interference causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege distinctly interference with contract and/or interference with prospective advantage causes of action. The demurrers to the remaining causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts to support those claims and defendant raises factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Plaintiff shall have 20 days to amend. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
